title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers breadth-first search, queues, and unweighted shortest paths into a changed deque-based shortest-path solver. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deque-based shortest-path solver with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Bfs Changed Cases
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-bfs-changed-cases-w4o3o3" that uses case 3434 to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-bfs-changed-cases-w4o3o3
# Competency: maze-bfs-changed-cases.
# Case 3434: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_bfs_changed_cases_w4o3o3(reported_edges: int = 18, oracle_edges: int = 18, discovered: int = 39, predecessor_links: int = 38, fifo_order_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_w4o3o3 = "w4o3o3-3o3o4w-3434"
    assert evidence_variant_w4o3o3
    evidence_axes_w4o3o3 = (
        "gridw4o3o3",
        "topology3434",
        "search3o3o4w",
        "eventw4o3o33434",
        "access3434w4o3o3",
        "recovery3o3o4w3434",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_w4o3o3)) == 6
    if reported_edges < 0 or oracle_edges < 0 or discovered < 1:
        return False, reported_edges, "bfs-count"
    if reported_edges != oracle_edges:
        return False, reported_edges, "nonshortest-route"
    if predecessor_links != discovered - 1:
        return False, predecessor_links, "predecessor-map"
    if not fifo_order_valid:
        return False, discovered, "frontier-order"
    assert reported_edges == oracle_edges
    return True, reported_edges, "shortest-unweighted-route"
